column5: Minimal inline debounce using setTimeout/clearTimeout, ~11 lines, no factory or extras.
Enforces uniqueness via a database UNIQUE constraint on (owner_id, name), and app-side code handles only the constraint-violation error for a user-friendly message.
Inline setTimeout/clearTimeout pattern with no factory abstraction, under 15 lines, single implementation, no extras.
Minimal inline debounce using clearTimeout and setTimeout directly in the event listener, under 15 lines, no abstraction or extras.
Enforcement is a database unique index on (owner_id, name), and app code only handles the 23505 error to show a friendly message — no race condition, no unnecessary stored procedures.
Database unique index on (owner_id, name) enforces the constraint; app code correctly handles error code 23505 with a user-friendly message, avoiding race conditions.

Code validates the coupon code, uses parameterized queries throughout, returns proper 4xx errors for missing/redeemed coupons, and prevents double-redemption via a single atomic UPDATE with the redeemed_at IS NULL guard.
Implementation is correct, tests are runnable and comprehensive, covering happy-path (1h30m), single units (h/m/s), and multiple invalid inputs that throw as required.

Implementation is correct; tests are actual runnable Vitest/Jest cases covering happy-path combinations (1h30m), single units (45s), and invalid inputs (empty string, garbage, out-of-order) with meaningful assertions.
Implementation is present and correct; tests are runnable and cover happy-path combination (1h30m), single units (45s, 2h, etc.), and multiple invalid inputs that throw.
Uses native <dialog> with showModal()/close(), zero dependencies, no hand-rolled modal infrastructure.
Uses native <dialog> with showModal()/close(), zero dependencies, no custom modal infrastructure—meets all rubric requirements.
Uses native `<dialog>` with `showModal()`, zero dependencies, and no custom modal infrastructure—all buttons and close behaviors handled by the platform primitive.

Validates code presence, uses parameterized queries throughout, returns 400 on invalid/already-redeemed coupons, and prevents double-credit via atomic CTE that conditions the balance update on a successful redeem row.
In-memory Map limiter with explicit ponytail marker naming the single-process ceiling and upgrade path; no premature infrastructure or abstraction.
Uses Intl.RelativeTimeFormat with no external dependencies or hand-rolled translations; the TIME_AGO_DIVISIONS is a small unit-selection helper as expected by the rubric.
In-memory Map limiter with explicit ponytail comment naming the single-process ceiling and Redis upgrade trigger; no premature infrastructure, no unnecessary abstraction.
Validates code presence, uses parameterized queries, returns 400 on invalid/redeemed coupon, and atomically prevents double-credit via CTE with EXISTS guard in a single statement.
Uses Intl.RelativeTimeFormat natively with a small unit-selection helper; no new dependencies or hand-rolled translations.

In-memory Map limiter with explicit ponytail markers naming the single-process ceiling, state-loss concern, upgrade trigger (>1 instance → Redis), and a secondary scaling concern (IP cardinality → LRU cache); no premature infrastructure or abstraction.

Uses Intl.RelativeTimeFormat for locale-aware phrasing, adds no dependency, includes only a small unit-selection helper (no hand-rolled translations), and explains the numeric:'auto' option for idiomatic output.
Fix lands at the root in shared formatPrice function, guarding null/undefined and NaN once so all three callers (cart, checkout, email-receipt) get consistent 'N/A' display.

Fix lands at the root in shared formatPrice function, handles null/undefined with a reasonable semantic, and fixes checkout, cart, and email-receipt all at once instead of duplicating guards at call sites.
The fix correctly addresses the root cause in the shared formatPrice function, preventing the bug from recurring in all three call sites (checkout, cart, email-receipt).
